Interface MouseEvent

All Superinterfaces:
Event, InputEvent
All Known Subinterfaces:
MouseEvent.MouseButtonEvent
All Known Implementing Classes:
MouseEvent.MouseClickedEvent, MouseEvent.MouseCursorMovedEvent, MouseEvent.MousePressedEvent, MouseEvent.MouseReleasedEvent, MouseEvent.MouseScrolledEvent
public sealed interface MouseEvent extends InputEvent permits MouseEvent.MouseButtonEvent, MouseEvent.MouseCursorMovedEvent, MouseEvent.MouseScrolledEvent
Base interface for events representing a mouse/cursor user input.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static interface 
    MouseEvent.MouseButtonEvent
    Base interface for events representing a mouse button state (pressed/released) change.
    static final record 
    MouseEvent.MouseClickedEvent
    This event is fired when a mouse button was clicked on a stage, i.e.
    static final record 
    MouseEvent.MouseCursorMovedEvent
    This event is fired when mouse cursor was moved on a stage.
    static final record 
    MouseEvent.MousePressedEvent
    This event is fired when a mouse button was pressed on a stage.
    static final record 
    MouseEvent.MouseReleasedEvent
    This event is fired when a mouse button was released on a stage.
    static final record 
    MouseEvent.MouseScrolledEvent
    This event a fired when one of the mouse wheels were scrolled on a stage.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default Point2D
    position()
    Returns the position on the stage where this event was performed.
    Stage
    stage()
    Returns the stage on which the event was done.
    double
    x()
    Returns the X coordinate on the stage where this event was performed.
    double
    y()
    Returns the Y coordinate on the stage where this event was performed.

  • Method Details

    • stage

      Stage stage()
      Returns the stage on which the event was done.
      Specified by:
      stage in interface InputEvent
      Returns:
      the target stage

    • x

      double x()
      Returns the X coordinate on the stage where this event was performed. 0 is leftmost coordinate.
      Returns:
      the X coordinate

    • y

      double y()
      Returns the Y coordinate on the stage where this event was performed. 0 is topmost coordinate.
      Returns:
      the Y coordinate

    • position

      default Point2D position()
      Returns the position on the stage where this event was performed.
      Returns:
      the position
